title: Star Atlas Relay Program (Pilot)
brief: This proposal seeks up to 13,143,639 ATLAS in Ecosystem Fund funding to run a time- and budget-bounded pilot that incentivizes authentic, player-created short-form Star Atlas gameplay videos and distributes the strongest submissions through official/community social channels, using a human review process and a lightweight web platform for submissions, scoring, and weekly leaderboards.
description:
# 1. Star Atlas Relay Program (Pilot)

**Category:** Funding
**Author:** Funcracker (Star Atlas community member; liaison to Aephia Industries)

---

# 2. SUMMARY

This proposal seeks **up to 13,143,639 ATLAS** in Ecosystem Fund funding to run a **time- and budget-bounded pilot** that incentivizes authentic, player-created short-form Star Atlas gameplay videos and distributes the strongest submissions through official/community social channels, using a human review process and a lightweight web platform for submissions, scoring, and weekly leaderboards.

---

# 3. MOTIVATION

Star Atlas already has a rare marketing asset: **real gameplay with high visual fidelity** that “sells itself” when shown to new audiences.

However:
- Marketing budgets are limited
- Traditional paid marketing often performs poorly in Web3 contexts
- Authentic community-created content tends to outperform paid promotion in terms of trust and credibility

This pilot tests a structured alternative: a **community-powered content pipeline** where creators submit real gameplay clips, reviewers filter and score them, and only content that has been sufficiently reviewed is considered for reposting and rewards. The goal is to generate consistent outward visibility while keeping overhead low and maintaining credibility.

This approach also enables the creation of dedicated **@StarAtlasRelay distribution channels**, allowing the ecosystem to consistently showcase real gameplay across platforms even when individual creators do not yet have an established audience.

---

# 4. SPECIFICATION

## 4.1 Definitions of key terms or concepts

* **Star Atlas Relay Program (SARP):** A pilot initiative to collect, review, and amplify authentic Star Atlas gameplay clips created by community members.
* **Submission:** A single gameplay video clip submitted via the program website (https://atlasrelay.io), optionally including links to the same clip posted on social platforms.
* **Reviewer:** A whitelisted Discord account authorized to score submissions and determine repost eligibility.
* **Daily Video Bounty (“Daily Quest”):** A fixed reward granted to a user’s first eligible submission each UTC day.
* **Performance Bonus:** A one-time bonus calculated 7 days after submission based on reviewer score and (if available) performance metrics.
* **z.ink mention bonus:** A fixed bonus that is applied if reviewers confirm a clear, accurate reference to the z.ink airdrop campaign in the submission.
* **Review threshold:** A submission becomes eligible for repost queueing and reward processing once it has received at least **three** reviews, with an average score >= 1.

## 4.2 Proposed Changes/Actions

**A) Launch a pilot phase (1–2 months)**
- Operate a structured program where players submit short gameplay clips (between 10–60s).
- Reviewers score submissions daily, and through their scores, gate which clips will be queued for reposting.
- Run weekly leaderboards (Sunday 00:00 UTC reset).


**B) Establish a review-gated distribution workflow**
* Submissions that pass the review threshold become eligible for distribution.
* Distribution may occur through two complementary paths:
  * **Resharing existing creator posts** when the submitter has already published the clip and provided the link.
  * **Publishing the clip directly via official @StarAtlasRelay accounts** when no external post is provided, or when additional amplification is warranted.
* Reposting remains editorial/selective even after eligibility (not every eligible clip must be posted).
* The intent is for the program itself to become a reliable, ongoing source of short-form Star Atlas gameplay content across platforms.

For the duration of the pilot phase:
* The @StarAtlasRelay distribution accounts are operated by the proposer (Funcracker).
* Ownership may be transferred to the Council at its request.
* Editorial authority is exercised collectively through the reviewer threshold system defined in this PIP.

Posting cadence is intentionally flexible and determined operationally based on submission volume, review timing, and platform best practices. Automation is preferred. If automation is not feasible, the proposer assumes responsibility for manual posting, with optional volunteer assistance from reviewers.

**C) Implement an incentive structure funded by the Ecosystem Fund**
- Daily bounty for consistent participation (anti-spam rules included).
- Bonus reward based on the quality of the work after a 7-day evaluation window.

**D) Provide the web platform required to run the pilot**
- A submission and review website (Discord authentication, whitelisting for reviewers, storage of video + metadata, leaderboards).
- Aephia Industries will build and host the platform at **no cost to the DAO** during the pilot.

## 4.3 Content Requirements (Eligibility)

**Eligible content**
- Short-form Star Atlas gameplay videos (10–60 seconds)
- Clips that are understandable to outsiders (scale, immersion, action, “wow moments”)

**Not allowed**
- AI-generated gameplay footage, or anything that could reasonably be confused with real Star Atlas gameplay visuals.

**Allowed AI usage (presentation only)**
- Voiceover, subtitles, edits/overlays, on-screen presenter/avatar.

## 4.4 Submission Requirements

Submissions are made through the program website and include:
- Discord login (required)
- Creator display name (used for attribution - optional)
- A Solana wallet address for payouts
- Video upload (original file)
- Title
- Optional short description
- Optional social links (X, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, Instagram Reels)

By submitting, creators grant the Star Atlas DAO, ATMTA, and program-operated distribution accounts permission to repost, remix, compile, crop, caption, or otherwise edit the submitted video for promotional purposes, **with required attribution** to the creator. Attribution will use the creator’s chosen display name (or fall back to their Discord name).

**Distribution logic based on submission method**
- If a creator provides links to posts they have already published, the program may **reshare, remix, or incorporate that content into compilation videos**, in addition to amplifying the original post, with attribution.
* If no external links are provided, the program may **publish the video directly** through its own **@StarAtlasRelay accounts** on supported platforms, with attribution to the creator.
This ensures that:
* Creators with audiences retain ownership of their reach
* Creators without audiences still gain exposure
* High-quality content can be amplified consistently regardless of the creator’s size

## 4.5 Review & Scoring Rules

**Review team (initial)**
- Virtuwul
- Ziggy
- Odvb
- Funcracker
- All five Council members (of the 3rd Council / SAC) 

> All named parties have explicitly consented to participate in their assigned roles at the time of submission of this PIP.

**Scoring UI**
- ❌ = -1 (disqualify)
- 🥉 = 1 (nice, but not great. Add to the backlog and post when there is a free slot)
- 🥈 = 2 (super decent stuff, nice work)
- 🥇 = 3 (astounding, this is the stuff we should push far and wide!)

The review interface will include an additional toggle, “z.ink reference present,” that allows the reviewer to signal that a relevant z.ink reference is present in the video.

**Review flow**
- Reviewers see unreviewed videos prioritized by those with fewer than 3 total reviews.
- Reviewers can revisit their past reviews and adjust them.
- Once a submission has ≥3 reviews, with an average review score of 1 or higher, it becomes **eligible** for repost queueing and reward processing.

## 4.6 Rewards (ATLAS-only for this pilot)

**Daily Video Bounty (“Daily Quest”)**
- Every UTC day, the first submitted (and approved by 3+ reviewers) video by a user earns **10,000 ATLAS**.
- Additional submissions on the same UTC day are allowed and reviewed, but do **not** qualify for the daily bounty. They do qualify for the bonus reward.

> Daily eligibility is determined by the **UTC calendar day of submission**, not by the day reviews are completed. If approval occurs later, the reward is applied retroactively to the original submission day.

**Performance Bonus (7-day evaluation)**
- Seven days after submission, a one-time bonus is awarded:
  - Base range: **10,000 – 45,000 ATLAS**
  - Plus **+5,000 ATLAS** if a z.ink airdrop campaign mention is confirmed by reviewers. This z.ink bonus is confirmed when a majority of reviewers for that submission mark the z.ink reference toggle.
  - Max total bonus: **50,000 ATLAS**
- Total max per “top-notch” submission day:
  - 10,000 (daily) + 45,000 (max performance) + 5,000 (z.ink) = **60,000 ATLAS**

Our aim is to determine the performance bonus scoring uses a hybrid model:
* **60% reviewer score**
* **40% platform performance metrics (views/engagement)**
However, if platform metrics cannot be reliably accessed, performance scoring defaults to **100% reviewer score**. As we can make no guarantees regarding the feasibility of accurately monitoring views (at this time), this fallback should be assumed for the evaluation of this proposal.

**Reviewer participation rule**
- Reviewers are eligible for the **daily bounty** on their own submissions.
- Reviewers are **not eligible** for **performance bonus rewards** on their own submissions, to reduce conflicts of interest.

**Important note on automation**
- Rewards are expected to be paid manually from the multisig during the pilot phase.
- Aephia may research payout automation after launch, but automation is not required for the pilot phase.

---

# 5. STAKEHOLDER IMPACT

* **Star Atlas Players / Creators (positive):**
  - Earn consistent rewards for gameplay clips; increased visibility for creators with smaller audiences.
* **Star Atlas DAO / Ecosystem Fund (positive):**
  - Tests a low-overhead, performance-driven alternative to traditional marketing spend.
* **ATMTA / Official marketing presence (positive):**
  - Receives a curated pipeline of community-created gameplay content that can be reposted selectively.
* **Review Team (mixed):**
  - Positive: community leadership + fun role.
  - Negative: recurring time commitment; risk of community pressure/disputes over scoring decisions.
  - Neutral: responsibility
* **PVP holders / DAO voters (mixed):**
  - Positive: measurable experiment with a bounded budget; transparent process.
  - Negative: risk of low ROI if distribution doesn’t translate into new users/attention; potential perception of “paying for content.”
* **Bad actors/spam submitters (negative):**
  - Review-gating and whitelist access reduce the ability to exploit the program.

---

# 6. IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS

## Step-by-step execution (chronological)

1. **Deploy the Star Atlas Relay web platform (https://atlasrelay.io)**
   - Hosting: Cloudflare (Pages + Workers; video storage via Cloudflare Stream; metadata in D1 & KV).
   - Features:
     - Discord authentication for users and reviewers
     - Reviewer whitelist gating for the review page
     - Video submission page
     - Review dashboard with scoring + z.ink toggle
     - Weekly leaderboard prominently featured on landingpage
2. **Establish reward payment operations**
   - Set up a 5/9 **Squads multisig** for reward disbursement operations.
   - After PIP approval, the DAO treasury transfers funding to the multisig (released per milestones per this PIP).
   - Define internal cadence for manual payments (e.g., daily or batched).
3. **Run the pilot phase**
   - The review team reviews daily.
   - Eligible videos are queued for distribution through:
     * Resharing creator-posted content where links are provided
     * Direct posting via official program-operated **@StarAtlasRelay accounts** across supported platforms
   * Distribution may initially be manual and later partially automated, depending on feasibility.
   - Rewards are issued according to daily bounty and 7-day bonus evaluations.
   - Weekly leaderboard resets on Sundays at 00:00 UTC.
4. **Reporting & wrap-up**
   - Provide transparent public reporting:
     - number of submissions
     - number reviewed
     - rewards distributed
     - remaining budget
   - End after 2 months or when funds are depleted.
   - Return the remaining ATLAS to the DAO treasury.

## Potential challenges

* **Spam / low-quality submissions**
  - Mitigation: review-gating + 3-review threshold + manual queueing; daily-bounty rule prevents “multi-submit farming.”
* **Bias / conflict-of-interest concerns**
  - Mitigation: multiple reviewers per submission; reviewers ineligible for their own performance bonuses.
* **Operational overhead**
  - Mitigation: simple scoring UI, clear prioritization; weekly windows.
* **Cross-platform posting complexity**
  - Pilot phase assumes reposting can start manually or with minimal tooling; automation research is separate. If paid third-party services are required, any costs must be agreed upon by the multisig and are deducted from the total reward pool.
* **Distribution tooling costs**
  - Mitigation: A hard cap equivalent to **$200 USD in ATLAS** is reserved for third-party posting or automation tools if required. These costs are deducted from the reward pool, must be approved by the multisig, and will be publicly reported. If the remaining multisig balance reaches the amount required to cover incurred tooling costs, the program halts.

## Hosting solution (off-chain service)

- Cloudflare Pages + Cloudflare Workers
- Cloudflare Stream for video
- Cloudflare KV + R2 for metadata/snapshots

---

# 7. TIMELINE

As soon as this proposal is approved, work begins to achieve our first milestone. Funding is expected only after we have clearly demonstrated to the Council that this milestone has been achieved. 

## Milestone 1 — Pilot launch readiness (Website + Ops Live)
**Deliverables**
- Submission + review website live (Discord auth + whitelist + leaderboard)
- Squads 5/9 multisig established and operational
- Review team ready; program rules published on site

**Target completion**
- Within **7 days after approval**

**Payment**
- **13,143,639 ATLAS** released upon milestone verification (website live + multisig setup + reviewer access confirmed)

## Milestone 2 — Month 1 completion report
**Deliverables**
- Public report covering the first month of operation:
  - number of submissions, reviews, and rewards issued
  - budget remaining
  - operational issues + fixes
- The program remains functional and actively reviewed

**Target completion**
- **30 days after Milestone 1**

**Estimated completion date**
- The pilot phase ends **1–2 months** after kickoff (or earlier if the budget is depleted).

## External Dependencies
- **Discord OAuth availability** (for login)
- **Cloudflare services availability** (Pages/Workers/Stream/KV/R2)
- **Operational access to repost accounts** (manual posting initially; automation research later)

---

# 8. COST & RESOURCE ALLOCATION

## One-time Costs

* **DAO Treasury / Ecosystem Fund:** Up to **13,143,639 ATLAS** total pilot budget.  
  The overwhelming majority of this pool is reserved for participant rewards.  
  A hard-capped reserve equivalent to $200 USD in ATLAS may be deducted from this same pool for approved distribution tooling costs.  
* **Aephia Industries (non-monetary):** Development effort for the platform — Provided at no cost during the pilot phase.

## Ongoing Costs

* **DAO Treasury:** N/A for the pilot phase (no ongoing funding requested beyond the pilot reward pool).
* **Aephia Industries (monetary):** Hosting costs during the pilot phase (Cloudflare services) — Provided at no cost during the pilot phase.
* **Aephia Industries (non-monetary):** Maintenance during the pilot phase — Provided at no cost for the pilot phase duration.
* **Post-pilot:** If successful and extended, ongoing hosting/maintenance costs will be transparently tallied and proposed in a follow-up PIP. There is no implied obligation for the DAO to reimburse Aephia Industries retroactively; any future funding would require a separate, independently approved proposal.
* **Distribution tooling reserve:** Up to the ATLAS equivalent of **$200 USD** may be used for third-party posting or automation tools if required. These costs are deducted from the reward pool, require multisig approval, and will be transparently reported.

## Alternative funding sources considered

- N/A for participant rewards (the pilot is explicitly structured as an Ecosystem Fund experiment).

Aephia Industries is covering development and hosting costs during the pilot phase.  
The only exception is the capped distribution tooling reserve (up to $200 USD equivalent in ATLAS), which is authorized to be deducted from the same pilot budget if third-party posting or automation tools are required and approved by the multisig.

---

# 9. ADDITIONAL DETAILS

* **Conflicts of interest:**
  - Funcracker is the proposer and a member of the review team, and is the liaison to Aephia Industries (which is building/hosting the platform).
  - Mitigations:
    - Multiple reviewers per submission
    - Reviewers cannot receive performance bonuses on their own submissions
    - Transparent leaderboard and review records

* **Earlier versions:** N/A (this is the first official draft PIP; prior ideation and community discussion informed this draft as expected)
